summary.intent: Package the Gossamer Electron app for distribution via Homebrew, including bundling the server inside the app and automating DMG builds via GitHub Actions.
summary.outcome: Set up electron-builder with GitHub Actions CI to build macOS DMGs, created a Homebrew tap repo with a Cask formula, and added an auto-update script to patch the Cask with new SHA256s on release. The CI pipeline was partially debugged but not fully verified green by end of session.

repo: The project has two separate package.json files: root (gossamer-serve Node.js server) and app/ (Electron frontend) — they were originally designed to run independently.
server.ts uses import.meta.url to resolve PROJECT_ROOT for Prisma binary paths, which constrains how the server can be bundled (directory structure must be preserved).
prisma was in devDependencies but is required at runtime for pushSchema calls — must be in production dependencies.
The Electron app detects dev vs prod mode via presence of ELECTRON_RENDERER_URL environment variable.
utilityProcess.fork() requires a CJS entry point, but the server is ESM — requires a CJS wrapper that dynamically imports the ESM server.
The git repo root is at gossamer/, but electron-builder runs from app/ and cannot auto-detect the GitHub repository from .git/config because it's in the parent directory.

code.finding: Modified to spawn gossamer-serve as a utilityProcess in production, using server-launcher.cjs as the fork target inside Contents/Resources/server/

Added electron-builder build config with extraResources pointing to ../dist, ../node_modules, ../prisma, ../package.json using ../ relative paths because server lives in root, not app/. Also requires repository field for electron-builder publish detection.
CJS bridge file created to allow utilityProcess.fork() (CJS-only API) to launch the ESM server via dynamic import()

Workflow must use --publish never with electron-builder to prevent it from trying to publish itself; upload is handled separately by softprops/action-gh-release. Also includes a step to auto-update Cask SHA256s in homebrew-gossamer repo.
prisma moved from devDependencies to dependencies so npm prune --production retains it for runtime use

workflow: electron-builder auto-detects GitHub repo from .git/config only when run from the repo root; subpackages need an explicit repository field in their package.json.
electron-builder's publish behavior defaults to skipping uploads on non-tag builds unless --publish always is set; use --publish never to build-only and handle uploads in a separate CI step.
Fine-grained GitHub tokens for pushing to a tap repo only need Contents: Read and write + Metadata: Read-only on the target repo.
Native modules (better-sqlite3, node-pty) must be rebuilt against Electron's ABI using @electron/rebuild before packaging with electron-builder.
Worktrees can be used to commit to a different branch without checking it out, but the worktree must be created from the correct remote ref, not the local branch.

friction: package-lock.json was out of sync with package.json after adding electron-builder, causing npm ci to fail in CI — required running npm install locally and committing the updated lock file.
All packaging changes were on the README branch, not main, making it awkward to push fixes — had to use git worktrees to commit to README branch without switching.
Network connectivity issues prevented pushing commits at end of session.
The CI branch trigger had to be temporarily changed from tags to branch pushes to test the workflow without cutting a real release.
electron-builder tried to auto-publish DMGs itself (conflicting with the softprops upload step) because publish mode was not explicitly set to never.
open_items: Revert CI workflow trigger from branch push back to tags: ['v*'] once build is confirmed green.
Add HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret to gossamer repo settings (fine-grained token with Contents: write on homebrew-gossamer).
App is unsigned — users will hit macOS Gatekeeper on first launch. Long-term fix requires Apple Developer account ($99/yr) and signing secrets in CI.
Push README.md changes (Homebrew install instructions) to main — was written locally but not committed/pushed due to network issues.
Consider consolidating app/ and root into a single package in the future — the current split requires ../relative extraResources paths and dual build orchestration in CI.
Verify the full CI pipeline passes end-to-end and that the Cask auto-update script correctly patches SHA256s in homebrew-gossamer.
